reasoning: While the Trump administration officially 'shut down' and removed the content of Climate.gov to comply with an Executive Order in late June 2025, the actual domain was not taken offline. Instead, the domain was configured to redirect to noaa.gov (or noaa.gov/climate). Because the resolution criteria STRICTLY rely on the automated checking site 'Is It Down Right Now?', which determines uptime by checking if the server responds to pings/HTTP requests, a functioning redirect (HTTP 301/302) is interpreted as the server being 'UP and reachable'. Since Reddit users confirmed on June 28, 2025, that the domain was explicitly 'still up' and redirecting just days before the resolution date, 'Is It Down Right Now?' almost certainly evaluated it as UP on July 1, 2025, triggering a YES resolution despite the spirit of the site being defunct. I leave a 12% margin for the possibility of an admin annulment or a temporary SSL/DNS misconfiguration on the day of the check.
